name: 1997 Iranian Presidential Election
description: The 1997 Iranian presidential election resulted in a landmark victory for reformist candidate Mohammad Khatami, who won approximately 70% of the vote against establishment favourite Ali Akbar Nategh-Nouri. The election shocked the Iranian political establishment and is considered the beginning of the reformist era. It demonstrated the significant gap between popular preferences and the ruling establishment's expectations.
editorial_notes: Often called the '2nd Khordad' election (23 May falls on 2 Khordad in the Persian calendar). Turnout was approximately 80%.
